title: acssuite must ALWAYS write acs-verdict.json — compile-fail/zero-discovery must produce a red verdict WITH the real reason, never a missing file
why: Cycle-949: the cycle's ACS predicate package failed to COMPILE (undefined: core.WithJudgeModel — tdd encoded a seam the builder never implemented). acssuite discovered 0 predicates; generateACSVerdict (audit.go:497-501) deliberately writes NOTHING on Total==0 so the missing-file floor FAILs the cycle. The floor held, but the user-visible defect string was 'acs-verdict.json: read: no such file or directory' — the REAL cause (predicate compile error) was buried in retro logs. Diagnosability failure: every operator/orchestrator layer above saw an opaque infra-looking FAIL and could not distinguish 'predicates red' / 'predicates never compiled' / 'suite crashed'. Same missing-file opacity would fire on any acssuite.Run error.
how_to_apply: Root-cause redesign, not workaround: acssuite.Run must return a verdict object in ALL failure modes — (a) predicate package compile failure => verdict with red_count>=1 and a synthetic result {ac_id: cycleN/COMPILE, result: red, evidence_excerpt: <compiler stderr>}; (b) zero predicates discovered => verdict with an explicit zero_predicates:true marker (floor still FAILs — no auto-pass — but the reason is in the artifact); (c) generateACSVerdict always calls WriteVerdict. Update the audit gate + detectVerdictIncoherence to read the reason from the artifact. Keep the EGPS red_count==0 ship rule unchanged.
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-949/audit-fail-reason.json (sole reason = file-not-found); .evolve/runs/cycle-949/retrospective-stdout.log ('Root cause confirmed. The cycle-949 acs predicate package fails to compile: undefined: core.WithJudgeModel'); go/internal/phases/audit/audit.go:483-508
